If there be an ant-hi]I to the north of an Arjuna tree, there
would be water at a depth of 17J cubits at a distance of three cubits
to the west of the tree. Here the signs are that at a depth of 2\
cubits there will be a white alligator; then at a depth of five cubits,
grey clay; then dark, then yellow, then white, and then sandy earth;
and beneath that abundant water will be found.

If there is a Nirgundi tree overgrown with an ant-hill (or
with an ant-hill closeby), there will be sweet and never-falling
water at a depth of Hi cubits at a distance of three cubits to the
south of the tree. There will be red fish at a depth of 2J cubits; then,
red-brown clay; next, palc-white clay; then, sand mixed with gravel
